data.body:
ROUND 5 — 3 spikes (codebase + 2 web) on agent runtime + engine/format. Convergent. Reshapes architecture.

SPIKE A (codebase: agent vs non-agent turns):
- PRODUCTION rebar.llm has NO single-turn/no-tools path. EVERY LLM turn goes through LangGraph create_agent.
  Even "extract" mode builds an agent-with-tools then does a SEPARATE with_structured_output().invoke()
  extraction call (runner.py:152,318). So a single-turn criterion runs as a zero/low-tool AGENT — overkill.
- LangGraph SURFACE actually used: create_agent (ReAct loop) + ToolStrategy (forced tool_choice for
  structured output) + init_chat_model (provider-agnostic). NONE of LangGraph's graph/state/checkpoint
  machinery is used. rebar pays for the whole runtime to use the prebuilt loop.
- Dep weight: [agents] = langchain>=1, langgraph==1.2.5 (PINNED, version-fragile), langgraph-prebuilt,
  langgraph-checkpoint, langchain-anthropic, langchain-openai, langchain-mcp-adapters, langfuse, pydantic,
  deepagents -> ~80-110 transitive deps. Tracing = Langfuse via LangChain CallbackHandler (tightly coupled).
- Runner seam (Runner Protocol: run/preflight; Fake/LangGraph/DeepAgents) is clean conceptually, BUT tool +
  structured-output contracts are LangChain-specific -> a new runtime needs BRIDGING code, not a pure drop-in.

SPIKE B (agent runtime alternatives):
- The LangGraph rationale is DEAD: "native Langfuse tracing" is no longer unique in 2026 — Langfuse is
  OTel-native (OTLP ingestion) and the LangChain CallbackHandler is itself OTel-backed; Pydantic AI / OpenAI
  Agents SDK / smolagents / Claude Agent SDK all have first-class Langfuse integrations via OTel.
- Anthropic now has GA grammar-constrained structured output (output_format) + native MCP connector ->
  removes the ToolStrategy machinery rebar fights (forced tool_choice breaks extended-thinking).
- RECOMMEND: PYDANTIC AI (pydantic-ai-slim[anthropic,mcp]) — one framework covers BOTH single-turn typed
  calls AND tool-using agents; native MCP, decorator tools, first-class output_type, provider-agnostic w/
  Claude native, native OTel->Langfuse; much lighter; DELETES the ToolStrategy workaround + dual extraction
  path + fragile pinned trio. Runner-up: raw anthropic SDK + MCP SDK (lightest; ~100-200 LOC hand loop; own
  OTel; loses agnosticism). REJECT Claude Agent SDK (Claude-locked -> fails agnosticism; ~12s subprocess
  cold-start kills single-turn; 0.x alpha). Doc drift noted: docs/llm-framework.md claims prompts come from
  Langfuse get_prompt but prompts.py is git-canonical only.

SPIKE C (engine + format): FORMAT and ENGINE are INDEPENDENT axes.
- FORMAT: tailored LEAN declarative YAML/JSON IR (steps + if + loop + map), NOT a heavyweight standard
  (BPMN/ASL). Relaxed diff-noise does NOT rescue standards — their cost is the enterprise data model +
  agent-step impedance, not diffs.
- ENGINE: BUILD a thin synchronous interpreter (~300-600 LOC). Every off-the-shelf engine OWNS its run-state
  lifecycle, which CONFLICTS with rebar's event-sourcing (rebar IS the durability layer). Off-the-shelf field
  collapses: code-defined engines (Prefect/Dagster/Temporal/Airflow/Hatchet/Burr) fail the
  declarative-data/visual axes (Burr in-process but code-defined); Windmill is declarative+visual but
  server+Postgres+AGPL; ASL/DMN/CMMN/Serverless-Workflow have no maintained pure-Python in-process runtime;
  SCXML (python-statemachine) viable but no native dynamic fan-out; CWL steps are CLI tools (no in-process
  LLM). SpiffWorkflow+BPMN is the one serious contender (pure-Python, lxml-only, gateways/loops/multi-instance,
  best visual-editor story) BUT owns its run-state (serialize-whole-workflow blob) -> direct impedance with
  event-sourcing; heavy BPMN data model; no native agentic step; LGPL.
- NUANCE for Joe's novelty-skepticism: the thin interpreter is "good custom code" not "reinventing the wheel"
  — the off-the-shelf wheels don't fit the event-sourced AXLE. The reason to build is SPECIFIC and earned
  (rebar must own run-state); if that constraint were dropped, SpiffWorkflow is the only reconsideration.

CONVERGED STACK (provisional, to pressure-test): (1) agent runtime = Pydantic AI behind the existing Runner
seam (swap LangGraph); (2) engine = thin declarative interpreter over a tailored lean IR, run-state on the
event log; (3) visual = thin IR<->standard serializer + off-the-shelf embeddable editor (bpmn-js/draw.io),
ephemeral. Custom code confined to: the Runner adapter, the interpreter core, the IR<->editor serializer —
all "interface to rebar", except the interpreter which is justified by event-sourced run-state ownership.
